era_context: Iron Age II. Shalmaneser III continues his reign in Assyria. The Levant remains under Assyrian pressure. Israel, Aram-Damascus, and Phoenician city-states continue their regional politics. Egypt is fragmented under the late 22nd Dynasty. This period is characterized by relative stability in the Levant before the major political upheavals of the late 9th century BCE.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most regions outside the Assyrian sphere

events.title: Shalmaneser III's 9th regnal year campaign
events.description: Shalmaneser III's 9th regnal year campaign (c. 850 or 821 BCE depending on chronology) continues Assyrian military operations in the Levant. The campaign reinforces Assyrian control over vassal states and extracts tribute. The specific details are recorded in Assyrian annals, though the exact year assignment is uncertain.
events.certainty_note: The assignment of this campaign to 821 BCE is based on chronological reconstruction and is uncertain by ±5 years. The regnal year numbering is secure, but the absolute dating is debated.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified. The chronological assignment of Shalmaneser's campaigns remains uncertain.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 821 BCE is very sparse. No specific events are well-documented for this year outside of Assyrian military campaigns. The lack of documentation reflects the general sparsity of written records for most of the ancient world during this period.
